en.extract:
The grey-lored broadbill (Serilophus rubropygius) is a species of bird in the broadbill family, Eurylaimidae. It is found in Bangladesh, India, Bhutan, and Myanmar. It was previously also found in Nepal, but is now likely extinct there.

The '''grey-lored broadbill''' ('''''Serilophus rubropygius''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the broadbill family, [[Eurylaimidae]]. It is found in [[Bangladesh]], [[India]], [[Bhutan]], and [[Myanmar]].<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |editor-last=Gill |editor-first=Frank |editor2-last=Donsker |editor2-first=David |editor3-last=Rasmussen |editor3-first=Pamela |title=NZ wrens, broadbills & pittas |url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/nz_wrens/ |access-date=2023-11-30 |website=IOC World Bird List v13.2 |language=en-US}}</ref> It was previously also found in [[Nepal]], but is now likely extinct there.<ref>{{Citation |last=Kirwan |first=Guy M. |title=Gray-lored Broadbill (''Serilophus rubropygius'') |date=2023-10-24 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/sibbro2/1.0/introduction |work=Birds of the World |editor-last=Billerman |editor-first=Shawn M. |access-date=2023-11-30 |publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology |language=en |doi=10.2173/bow.sibbro2.01 |last2=Bruce |first2=Murray D. |editor2-last=Keeney |editor2-first=Brooke K. |editor3-last=Rodewald |editor3-first=Paul G. |editor4-last=Schulenberg |editor4-first=Thomas S. |url-access=subscription}}</ref>
